steps.title: Recognize the pattern
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, $x^2-144y^2$ is a difference of squares because $144y^2=(12y)^2$. This means the two factors must use the same two terms with opposite signs, so the middle terms cancel when expanded. The outside factor of $18$ stays attached after the inside expression is factored.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
Use $a^2-b^2=(a-b)(a+b)$ with $a=x$ and $b=12y$, then keep the outside factor of $18$.
$$18(x^2-144y^2)=18(x^2-(12y)^2)$$
$$18(x^2-144y^2)=18(x-12y)(x+12y)$$
The answer is B.
